name: Claude Free / ChatGPT Free / Gemini
description: AI writing, research, content drafting, business planning. Free tiers sufficient for most SME needs. Using more than one gives a second opinion on important drafts.

training.name: YouTube: AI for small business beginners

when: Every client. Universal.

name: Microsoft Copilot Chat
description: Free AI chat via Bing/Edge. If M365 subscriber, in-app Copilot keeps data in tenant but is a separate paid add-on (~£15-18/user/month), not counted as free value.
notes: Free Copilot Chat is separate from the paid M365 Copilot add-on.
when: M365 subscriber. Paid Copilot is separate product.

name: Canva Free
description: Professional design for social graphics, presentations, flyers, branded documents. Templates make it accessible. Substantial free capability.
when: Every client. Universal.

name: Adobe Express Free
description: Comparable to Canva. Slightly more polished output for professional documents. Worth comparing both. Free tier generous.
when: Client already in Adobe ecosystem or wants alternative to Canva.

name: GIMP
description: Open-source image editor. Professional-grade alternative to Adobe Photoshop. Layers, masks, filters. Steeper learning curve than Canva but unlimited capability.
notes: Paid alternatives: Adobe Photoshop, Affinity Photo.
when: Client needs serious image editing beyond Canva.

name: Photopea.com
description: Free browser-based Photoshop alternative. Opens PSD files, layers, masks. No install needed. Lower learning curve than GIMP if already familiar with Photoshop/Affinity.
notes: Paid alternatives: Adobe Photoshop, Affinity Photo/Designer.
when: Design/print trades, clients with PSD files or Photoshop experience.

name: Unsplash / Pexels / Pixabay
description: Free stock photography and video. High quality, no attribution required (check individual licences). Essential for website, social media, marketing materials.

training.name: YouTube: free stock images for business

notes: Suno/Udio AI music is NOT free for commercial use.
Each platform has its own search guides and licence terms.
when: Every client creating content.

name: Stock Music & Fonts
description: YouTube Audio Library, Uppbeat (uppbeat.io), Pixabay Music for royalty-free tracks. Google Fonts for web typography. Essential components of professional content at zero cost.

training.name: YouTube: royalty free music for business

when: Client producing video or web content.

name: Remove.bg / Cleanup.Pictures
description: Instant AI background removal (Remove.bg) and object removal from photos (Cleanup.Pictures). One-click fixes for common image problems. Free tiers limited but functional.

training.name: YouTube: remove background from image free
YouTube: remove objects from photo free

notes: Remove.bg free tier: 1 HD download per month.
Canva background remover is a Pro (paid) feature.
Paid alternatives: Adobe tools.
Each tool is largely self-explanatory.
when: Client with product photos, social media imagery needs.

name: TinyPNG / Squoosh
description: Compress images for website without visible quality loss. Faster site = better Google ranking. Also reduces email attachment sizes. Squoosh is Google's own tool.
notes: ImageOptim is Mac only.
Tools are largely self-explanatory.
when: Client with a website, especially Shopify/WooCommerce.

name: Vectorizer.ai / Coolors.co
description: Vectorizer: convert low-res logos to scalable vectors for printing. Coolors: extract exact hex/RGB from customer logos for brand matching. Niche but solves real problems.
notes: Adobe Illustrator Image Trace (paid) also vectorizes logos.
Each tool is largely self-explanatory.
when: Print, design, embroidery, signage trades.

name: Placeit by Envato
description: Show customers how designs look on actual garments/products before printing. T-shirt mockups, hoodies, caps. Essential for website product images. Some free, premium £7.95/download.
when: Print-on-demand, merch, garment businesses.

name: YouTube Studio
description: Free video hosting, distribution, analytics. No hosting cost. SEO benefits from Google ownership. Tutorials, testimonials, product demos, CPD.
notes: Instagram Reels is another social-first option for short video.
when: Client produces or plans to produce video.

name: CapCut
description: Mobile and desktop video editing with trending templates. Quick Reels, TikToks, social clips. Auto-captions, music, highlights. Low learning curve.
when: Client creating social media video content.

name: DaVinci Resolve
description: Professional-grade video editing, completely free. Industry-standard colour grading. Rivals Premiere Pro. Steeper learning curve but unlimited capability.
notes: Paid alternatives: Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro.
when: Serious video production (training, corporate, CPD).

name: OBS Studio
description: Open-source streaming and screen recording. Professional quality. Live streaming to YouTube/Facebook. Screen recording for tutorials and onboarding videos.
notes: Paid alternatives: Camtasia, ScreenFlow (Mac).
when: Client doing live streams, screen recordings, or tutorial creation.

name: Loom Free
description: Screen recording with face overlay. Record client onboarding, tutorials, walkthroughs. Simpler than OBS. Free tier: 25 videos, 5 min each.
notes: Paid alternative: Camtasia.
when: Client onboarding, workshop recording, async communication.

name: Opus Clip / Video Repurposing
description: Turn one long video/podcast into multiple social clips. AI finds highlight moments. Directly serves podcast-to-social workflows.
notes: Canva includes basic video trimming.
when: Client with podcast, long-form video, or webinar content.

name: Audacity
description: Open-source audio editor. Podcast editing, voiceover cleanup, noise removal, audio file conversion. Professional results at zero cost.
notes: Paid alternative: Adobe Audition.
when: Client producing podcasts or audio content.

name: GoPro Quik
description: Basic GoPro footage organisation and highlight reel generation. Auto-edits for social clips from action footage.
notes: Use DaVinci Resolve for serious edits.
when: Adventure, sports, outdoor, activity-based businesses.

name: Microsoft Clipchamp
description: Simple video editing included in M365. Social clips, basic cuts, transitions. Already owned if M365 subscriber.
notes: VN Video Editor (free) is another mobile option.
The Clipchamp blog offers further tutorials.
DaVinci Resolve (free) for serious editing.
when: M365 subscriber, simple video editing.

name: Google Analytics 4
description: Website analytics. Visitor behaviour, traffic sources, conversions. Free and should be on every business website. Takes 10 minutes to set up.
notes: Wix and Squarespace have built-in analytics.
when: Every client with a website.

name: Microsoft Clarity
description: Free session recordings and heatmaps. See what visitors actually do. Complements GA4. Unlimited traffic, no cap. Equivalent paid: Hotjar ~£32/month.
notes: Clarity's uncapped free traffic is rare among session recording tools.
when: Client has website worth analysing (e-commerce, lead-gen).

name: Google Search Console
description: SEO monitoring. Which search queries bring visitors, indexing status, technical issues. Essential for understanding how Google sees the site.
when: Every client with a website.

name: Bing Webmaster Tools
description: Bing/Copilot search visibility. ~15-20% UK search share. Often overlooked. Can import from Search Console. Also feeds Microsoft Copilot answers.
notes: Google Search Console is the primary tool; set that up first.
when: Client wanting broader search visibility, AEO readiness.

name: Google PageSpeed Insights / Lighthouse
description: Free website speed and performance testing. Core Web Vitals. Lighthouse (built into Chrome DevTools) for deeper audits. SEO ranking factor.
notes: Lighthouse is built into Chrome DevTools for deeper audits and debugging.
when: Website performance matters (e-commerce, SEO focus).

name: Google Tag Manager
description: Streamlines tracking setup. Add GA4, Clarity, Facebook Pixel without touching website code. Reduces developer dependency for marketing tracking.
notes: Most alternatives are paid at scale.
when: Client with multiple tracking tools or frequent pixel changes.

name: Google Looker Studio
description: Free dashboarding. Pull GA4, Search Console, Sheets data into visual reports. Auto-updating. Professional client reporting at no cost.
notes: Paid: Tableau, Power BI.
when: Client wanting automated reporting dashboards.

name: Schema.org / Merkle Schema Generator
description: Structured data markup for rich search results (star ratings, FAQs, events). Free industry standard. Equivalent paid: Schema App ~£100/month.

training.name: Google Search Central structured data docs

notes: WordPress plugins such as Rank Math and Yoast add schema automatically.
when: Client with website wanting rich search results.

name: Hotjar Free
description: Heatmaps and basic session recordings. More limited than Clarity (capped sessions) but well-established. Useful if client prefers Hotjar's interface.
when: Alternative to Clarity. Client may already have Hotjar.

name: Google Trends
description: Seasonal demand patterns, keyword trends, market intelligence. Free. Content planning, understanding when to push which services.
when: Marketing-focused client, seasonal business.

name: Google Ads Keyword Planner
description: Free keyword research even without running ads. Search volume, competition, content ideas. Requires Google Ads account (free to create, no spend needed).

training.name: YouTube: Google Keyword Planner tutorial no spend

notes: Keyword Surfer is a free Chrome extension for in-search volume data.
when: Client planning content strategy or considering paid ads.

name: AnswerThePublic
description: Keyword visualisation. Shows questions people ask about topics. Content ideation. 3 searches/day on free tier. Good for blog/FAQ planning.
notes: Largely self-explanatory.
Google's 'People Also Ask' boxes offer similar insight manually.
when: Client building content strategy, blog planning.

name: Ubersuggest
description: Free SEO tool. Keyword research, competitor analysis. Limited daily queries but useful for initial research before committing to SEMrush/Ahrefs.
notes: Keyword Surfer is a free Chrome extension.
Paid: Semrush, Ahrefs.
when: Client doing DIY SEO research.

name: Google Alerts
description: Free monitoring. Track competitor mentions, industry news, client company updates. Email alerts for any search term. Basic media monitoring at zero cost.

training.name: YouTube: Google Alerts for business monitoring

notes: Largely self-explanatory.
when: Competitor monitoring, brand awareness, industry tracking.

name: Google Scholar / ResearchGate
description: Free academic search (Scholar) and networking/publishing (ResearchGate). Essential for academic writing, CPD research, evidence-based practice.
when: Academic, CPD, research-based, health, education sectors.

name: Google Business Profile
description: Free listing in Google Search and Maps. Reviews, hours, photos, posts. Highest-impact free tool for any business with a local or physical presence.
when: Every client. Universal.

name: Bing Places for Business
description: Free Bing search listing. ~15-20% UK search share. Can import from Google Business Profile. Often overlooked, worth 5 minutes.
notes: Google Business Profile is the primary listing; set that up first.
when: Local service businesses, trades, retail.

name: Meta Business Suite
description: Free management for Facebook and Instagram. Schedule posts, analytics, messages, automated replies, appointment booking integration. Essential for social-active businesses.
when: Every client with social media.

name: LinkedIn (free profile)
description: Professional networking, credibility, B2B visibility. Free profile sufficient for most. Regular posting more valuable than Premium for most SMEs.
notes: The value is in the network and regular posting, not paid features.
when: Every client. Universal.

name: Buffer Free / Social Scheduler
description: Schedule posts across 3 channels, 10 posts queued. Free tier below paid Metricool/Hootsuite. Good trial before committing.

training.name: YouTube: Buffer tutorial social media scheduling

when: Client managing multiple social platforms.

name: Reddit (AEO channel)
description: AI-native discoverability. Genuine, non-marketing contributions answering questions seed AEO pickup by Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity. Not a broadcast channel; a credibility channel.
notes: LinkedIn articles offer a more professional voice.
Approach with authenticity, not marketing speak.
when: Clients interested in AEO / AI engine visibility.

name: Link-in-Bio tools
description: Single link page for Instagram/TikTok bio. Multiple links to website, booking, shop, socials. Free tiers on all major platforms.
notes: Each platform has its own setup guides.
Many website builders include a link-in-bio style page as a built-in feature.
when: Social-media-active businesses, especially Instagram/TikTok.

name: MailerLite Free / Mailchimp Free / Brevo Free
description: Email newsletters and automation. Free tiers: MailerLite (1,000 subscribers), Mailchimp (500 contacts), Brevo (300 emails/day). Landing pages, forms, basic automation.
notes: Listmonk is open source and self-hosted.
when: Client needs email marketing. Choose one.

name: HubSpot CRM Free
description: Free CRM, unlimited contacts. Pipeline, email integration, customer tracking. Generous free tier. Scalable.
notes: SuiteCRM is open source.
when: Client needs CRM and doesn't have one.

name: Zoho Free Ecosystem
description: Independent ecosystem: CRM (3 users), Bookings, Mail, Invoice, Writer, Meeting, Forms, all at zero cost. Genuine alternative to Google/Microsoft stacks, not just a CRM.

training.name: YouTube: Zoho free tools for small business

notes: SuiteCRM, EspoCRM and ERPNext are open source.
when: Client wanting integrated stack outside Google/Microsoft. Strong for sole traders.

name: Substack / Medium Free
description: Free newsletter/article publishing. Build subscriber list, share expertise. Substack: newsletter focus. Medium: existing readership. No cost until monetisation.
notes: LinkedIn Articles is free with any LinkedIn profile.
Ghost is open source; the hosted Ghost(Pro) service is paid.
when: Client publishing thought leadership or newsletters.

name: WhatsApp Business
description: Upgrade from personal WhatsApp. Quick replies for FAQs, product catalogue, away messages, business profile. Customers contact you the same way. Free.
when: Every client using WhatsApp for business (most Norfolk SMEs).

name: Calendly Free / Scheduling
description: Online appointment scheduling. One event type on free tier. Eliminates email back-and-forth for booking.
notes: Microsoft Bookings is included with M365 subscriptions.
Cal.com is open source; Zoho Bookings has a free tier.
when: Appointment-based business.

name: Notion Free / Trello Free
description: Project management and organisation. Notion: notes, wikis, databases. Trello: visual Kanban boards (Quote → Printing → Shipped). Both generous free tiers.
notes: Kanboard is open source and self-hosted.
Microsoft Planner is included with M365 subscriptions.
when: Client needing project/task management.

name: Zapier Free / Make.com Free / Apple Shortcuts
description: Automation glue. Connect apps: new form submission → add to spreadsheet → send email. Zapier free: 100 tasks/month. Make free: 1,000 ops/month. Apple Shortcuts for phone workflows.
notes: n8n is open source and can be self-hosted.
when: Client wanting workflow automation.

name: E-Signing Free Tiers
description: Digital signatures for contracts, engagement letters, consent forms. SignWell, Dokobit, Adobe Fill & Sign. Enough volume for a sole practitioner.

training.name: YouTube: free electronic signature tools UK

notes: Each platform has its own setup guides.
Zoho Sign has a free tier.
when: Professional services, legal, consultants needing signed documents.

name: QR Code Generation
description: Unlimited free QR codes. Link to booking pages, menus, feedback forms, product provenance pages. SVG export for print/CNC. Takes 30 seconds.

training.name: YouTube: QR codes for business UK

notes: Chrome has a built-in QR code generator in the address bar.
The tools are largely self-explanatory.
when: Physical-to-digital touchpoints: menus, events, products, feedback.

name: Hemingway Editor
description: Free writing clarity tool. Improves readability. Highlights complex sentences, passive voice, adverbs. Simpler alternative to Grammarly. Web-based, no install.
notes: The tool is self-explanatory.
AI assistants can also check and improve writing.
when: Client writing marketing copy, proposals, web content.

name: Phone Recording + Transcription
description: Apple Voice Memos, Google Recorder. Record meetings, site visits, client briefs. On-device transcription on newer phones. Pair with AI for notes/summaries.
notes: Google Recorder offers on-device auto-transcription on Android.
Microsoft Teams includes meeting transcription if on M365.
Voice Memos and Recorder apps are built into phones.
when: Client wanting to capture meetings, site visits, client conversations.

name: Gmail Business Features
description: Already available. Templates, scheduling, smart compose, filters, labels. Often underused. Can significantly improve email efficiency without any new tools.

training.name: YouTube: Gmail productivity tips for business

notes: Most email clients have similar features.
when: Client already on Gmail, not using features.

name: Zoom Basic / Google Meet
description: Free video conferencing. Zoom: 40-min group cap, unlimited 1:1. Google Meet: 60-min limit, no account needed for guests. Both screen share and record.
notes: Jitsi Meet is open source with no account needed.
when: Client needing video meetings, doesn't have Teams.

name: Spond
description: Staff/volunteer coordination and group comms. Popular with sports clubs, community groups, HAF providers. Event management, attendance tracking, subgroups.
notes: Doodle Premium and SignUpGenius Premium are paid tiers.
when: Sports clubs, community groups, HAF providers, volunteer orgs.

name: Microsoft Teams
description: Already available with M365. Meeting transcription, screen sharing, chat, channels. Could replace some WhatsApp usage for structured internal comms.

training.name: YouTube: Microsoft Teams for small business

notes: Rocket.Chat and Mattermost are open source and self-hostable.
when: M365 subscriber, internal team communication.

name: Wave Accounting
description: Free invoicing, accounting, receipt scanning. Bank connections, financial reports. Suitable for sole traders and micro-businesses.
notes: FreeAgent, Xero, QuickBooks and Sage are paid products.
when: Sole trader without accounting software.

name: FreeAgent Free via Mettle / Tide Free
description: Bank-linked accounting at no cost. FreeAgent free with Mettle or NatWest business accounts. Tide offers free invoicing and expense management. Replaces manual Excel returns.
notes: Xero, QuickBooks and Sage are paid products.
when: Sole trader with NatWest/Mettle account or willing to switch.

name: HMRC Tools (MTD, Self Assessment)
description: Free direct access: Self Assessment, tax records, MTD compliance. Required for VAT-registered businesses. Free bridging software available.

training.name: YouTube: HMRC webinars Making Tax Digital

notes: Statutory: HMRC filing itself has no substitute.
Free bridging software is available for MTD.
FreeAgent, Xero, QuickBooks and Sage are paid MTD-compatible options.
See the GOV.UK list of MTD-compatible software.
when: VAT-registered or approaching threshold. Self Assessment for all.

name: Bitwarden Free
description: Free, open-source password manager. Unlimited passwords, sync across devices. Eliminates weak/reused passwords. Implement immediately.
notes: 1Password and Dashlane are paid.
when: Every client. Non-negotiable.

name: Have I Been Pwned
description: Free data breach monitoring. Check business email addresses against known breaches. Check now, then periodically. Takes 30 seconds.

training.name: Have I Been Pwned site guide
YouTube: Have I Been Pwned how to use

notes: HIBP is the gold standard for breach checking.
when: Every client. Non-negotiable.

name: Google Password Checkup
description: Checks saved passwords against known breach databases. Quick 5-minute security audit. Built into Chrome. Complements HIBP.
when: Every client using Chrome. Quick addition to HIBP.

name: NCSC Cyber Essentials Guidance
description: Free security guidance from National Cyber Security Centre. Small Business Guide, Cyber Essentials self-assessment, Exercise in a Box. Authoritative and practical.

training.name: YouTube: Cyber Essentials for small business

notes: NCSC also offers free online training and Exercise in a Box cyber exercises via ncsc.gov.uk.
when: Every client. Universal.

name: ICO.org.uk GDPR Guidance
description: Free authoritative UK GDPR reference. Privacy notice templates, data protection self-assessment, DPIA guidance, registration (£35-60/yr). Definitive source. Statutory body.
notes: The ICO is a statutory body with no direct substitute; the alternatives listed are legitimate complements, not replacements.
The ICO also publishes plain-language direct marketing guidance on ico.org.uk.
when: Every client. Statutory.

name: Google Drive / Workspace Free
description: 15GB free. Docs, Sheets, Slides, Forms. Shareable, collaborative. Available with any Google account. Includes Google Slides for presentations.
notes: Free tiers: OneDrive 5GB (1TB with M365), Dropbox Basic 2GB, pCloud 10GB.
Nextcloud is open source and self-hosted.
when: Every client not already on M365.

name: OneDrive / Microsoft 365 Included
description: 1TB cloud storage included with M365. Auto-backup, file sharing, versioning. Already paid for if M365 subscriber. Needs enabling and configuring.
notes: Google Drive free tier is 15GB. Nextcloud is open source and self-hosted.
when: M365 subscriber. Already paid for; just enable.

name: Microsoft Lists / Planner
description: Task management and databases included in M365. Lists replaces spreadsheet-as-database. Planner for Kanban boards. Syncs with Teams.
notes: Kanboard and Taiga are open source.
when: M365 subscriber. Task tracking, project management.

name: Microsoft Bookings
description: Online booking included in M365. Clients self-book. Syncs Outlook. Already paid for if subscriber.
notes: Cal.com is open source and self-hostable.
when: M365 subscriber, appointment-based business.

name: Microsoft Forms + QR Codes
description: Surveys, feedback, consent capture. Data in M365 tenant. QR codes for physical-to-digital touchpoints.
notes: Jotform free tier allows 5 forms. LimeSurvey is open source.
when: M365 subscriber. Feedback, consent, event check-ins.

name: New Anglia Growth Hub / GoDigital Supplier Portal
description: Free, impartial business advice for Norfolk and Suffolk. GoDigital supplier portal for vetted digital service providers. Access to grants, finance, specialist advice.
when: Every Norfolk GoDigital client.

name: Norfolk Chamber of Commerce
description: Networking, events, business support resources. Free resources available. Membership for full benefits. Often overlooked by sole traders.

training.name: YouTube: Chamber of Commerce benefits for small business

when: Networking-interested clients, B2B businesses.

name: Free Training Academies
description: Structured free upskilling. HubSpot Academy (CRM, marketing), Meta Blueprint (social), Google Skillshop & Digital Garage (analytics, ads, digital), Xero U (accounting), SEMrush Academy (SEO).
notes: Each academy is free and self-serve.
when: Every client interested in upskilling. Recommend specific academies matching their needs.

name: Coursera / Khan Academy (audit mode)
description: Audit versions of professional courses available without certification cost. Khan Academy fully free. Business, economics, marketing, data, coding.

training.name: YouTube: free online courses business UK
YouTube: how to audit Coursera courses free

notes: Coursera and Khan Academy are self-serve; audit mode gives course content without certificates.
when: Client wanting structured learning beyond platform academies.

name: BridgeAI
description: UK-government-backed programme helping SMEs adopt and build AI-powered products. Advisor access, funded pilot projects, training. Low effort to apply.

training.name: YouTube: AI adoption small business UK

notes: Access can also be arranged via the New Anglia Growth Hub.
when: SMEs exploring AI adoption. Tech-forward clients.

name: Innovate UK Smart Grants / Gov.uk Grants Portal
description: Grant funding (non-dilutive) for UK SMEs pursuing R&D. Gov.uk grants portal aggregates available funding. Competitive but worth monitoring.

training.name: YouTube: Innovate UK Smart Grant application

when: R&D active businesses, innovation-led SMEs.

name: Nvidia Inception
description: Startup programme: cloud credits, go-to-market support, co-marketing, free training. Low effort to apply. Relevant for any AI-native business.
when: AI-native startups and tech businesses building on GPU/AI.

name: Shopify Dawn Theme + Shopify Learn
description: Free premium theme + comprehensive e-commerce education covering setup, photography, marketing, fulfilment. Dawn is fast, accessible, customisable.
notes: Dawn theme is free with any Shopify plan. WooCommerce is open source.
when: E-commerce businesses on or considering Shopify.

name: Amazon Seller Central
description: Secondary marketplace for digital guides, branded merch. Higher margins than Printful for some products. No seller account cost until listing.

training.name: YouTube: Amazon UK seller setup beginners

when: Merch, digital products, physical goods businesses.

name: Printful POD Integration
description: Low-risk print-on-demand merchandise. No inventory. Integrates with Shopify, Etsy, WooCommerce. Test brand appetite before committing to stock.

training.name: YouTube: print on demand UK beginners

when: Merch, branded goods, testing product-market fit.

name: Sketchup Free / Go
description: Entry-level 3D CAD for visualisations. Garden designs, room layouts, construction planning. Free web version, Go for iPad.
notes: Paid options: Sketchup Pro, AutoCAD, Vectorworks. Blender and FreeCAD are open source.
when: Garden/landscape, construction, interior design, architecture.

name: OPITO / GWO / HSE / Charities Commission Free Training
description: Sector-specific free training and compliance resources. Safety, emergency response, EDI, governance. Equivalent paid LMS content: £500-2,000/year.

training.name: YouTube: free health and safety training UK

notes: Sector specific: each body runs its own training portal and there are no direct cross-sector alternatives.
when: Safety training, energy sector, charities, regulated industries.

name: PostHog
description: Product analytics, session replay, feature flags, A/B testing. Open source. Generous free tier. For tech businesses building software products.
notes: PostHog is open source and self-hostable. For simpler needs, GA4 plus Microsoft Clarity covers most cases.
when: Tech/SaaS businesses building software products.
